rule: Use base verb for I-you-we-they and add s/es for he-she-it
example: She plays tennis every Saturday

rule: Use do not or does not plus base verb
example: He does not like spiders

rule: Use Do or Does at the beginning
example: Do you play chess

rule: Place adverb before main verb but after verb to be
example: She always arrives early
How many books have you got
How much water is there
How often do you visit your grandparents
How long does the journey take

rule: Use for singular or uncountable nouns
example: There is a cat in the garden
There are three books on the table
There are some apples in the basket
Have you got any brothers
There was a boy in the park
There were many people at the museum

rule: Use am is are plus verb ing for actions now
example: I am reading a comic
We are visiting grandma tomorrow

rule: Use in on at depending on time expression
example: I was born in April

rule: Use in on at depending on location
example: The keys are on the table

rule: My your his her its our their
example: This is her backpack

rule: Mine yours his hers ours theirs
example: The blue bike is mine
Tom's skateboard is new
The girl who won the race is my friend
The book which is on the desk is mine
This is the house where I grew up
The boy whose dog is missing is sad
